input: Write a product description for a noise-canceling headphone aimed at remote workers. Emphasize comfort for all-day wear and microphone quality for video calls. Keep it under 100 words.
expected: Compelling description focusing on comfort and mic quality. Specific about features (padding, weight, mic clarity). Under 100 words.
criteria: Under 100 words. Focuses on stated selling points (comfort, mic). Persuasive but not hyperbolic. Specific, not generic.

input: Write a commit message for a change that refactors the authentication middleware to use JWT tokens instead of session cookies, adds token refresh logic, and updates the OpenAPI spec.
expected: Concise commit message with imperative mood. Summary line under 72 chars. Body explains the why.
criteria: Imperative mood (Refactor, not Refactored). Summary under 72 chars. Body explains motivation. Mentions all three changes (middleware, refresh, OpenAPI).

input: Write a short README section explaining how to set up a development environment for a Python project that uses uv, Docker Compose, and a Taskfile.
expected: Step-by-step setup: install prerequisites, clone, uv sync, task setup, task dev. Clear and scannable.
criteria: Lists prerequisites. Step-by-step instructions. Uses code blocks for commands. Scannable format. No unnecessary prose.

input: Write a Slack message to your team celebrating the successful launch of a new feature. Keep it energetic but professional.
expected: Acknowledges the team effort, mentions the specific achievement, thanks contributors, expresses excitement. Brief.
criteria: Positive tone without being cringey. Specific about what launched. Acknowledges team. Under 80 words. Appropriate for Slack.

input: Write an executive summary (3-4 sentences) of the following situation: Your ML pipeline processing 10K daily predictions has seen a 15% accuracy drop over 2 weeks due to data drift in the income_bracket feature. The fix requires retraining with updated feature distributions.
expected: Concise summary covering: the problem (accuracy drop), the cause (data drift), the impact (10K daily predictions affected), and the fix (retrain).
criteria: 3-4 sentences max. Covers all four elements. No jargon executives wouldn't understand. Quantified impact. Clear action item.

input: Write a 404 error page message that is helpful and on-brand for a developer tools company. Include a suggestion for what the user can do next.
expected: Friendly but professional 404 message. Suggests going home, searching, or checking the URL. Maybe a subtle dev joke.
criteria: Helpful (suggests next actions). On-brand for devs (can include subtle humor). Not just 'Page not found'. Includes navigation suggestion.
